paper.title: Use of industrial by-products in concrete production
paper.authors: M. Etxeberria; E. Vazquez; A. Mari; M. Barra
paper.material_system: concrete with foundry sand, electric arc furnace slag, and blast furnace slag aggregates
paper.notes: 28-day stage strengths and high-temperature residual compressive strengths extracted from exact tables.

evidence.evidence_text: Stage 1 mix proportions for CC, C-QFS, C-GFS, C-EAFS25/50/100, C-BS25/50/100 list w/c total, aggregates, cement, water, and additives.
Stage 2 mix proportions for CC, C-QFS, C-GFS, C-EAFS25/50/100, C-BS25/50/100 list w/c total, aggregates, cement, water, and additives.

Hardened concrete properties of stage 1 at 28 d include compressive strength (MPa): CC 22.0, C-QFS 28.4, C-GFS 25.0, C-EAFS25 24.1, C-EAFS50 22.1, C-EAFS100 20.3, C-BS25 19.5, C-BS50 24.3, C-BS100 17.5.

Hardened concrete properties of stage 2 at 28 d include compressive strength (MPa): CC 39.3, C-QFS 33.3, C-GFS 30.5, C-EAFS25 47.1, C-EAFS50 46.3, C-EAFS100 54.1, C-BS25 41.0, C-BS50 46.5, C-BS100 47.8.

Residual compressive strength after exposure to 800 C during 4 h: before/after values for CC 60.8/23.1, C-QFS 59.6/24.4, C-GFS 50.7/23.5, C-EAFS25 63.8/33.2, C-EAFS50 64.5/32.6, C-EAFS100 75.1/33.3, C-BS25 57.8/27.5, C-BS50 63.5/33.6, C-BS100 68.0/34.6 MPa.
Stage 1 and stage 2 hardened properties were determined at 28 d of curing; stage 2 specimens were heated to 800 C for 4 h and then tested in compression.
